summary: Iteration 3 backend testing: 19/26 tests passed (73%). Two CRITICAL backend bugs found: (1) /api/auth/register returns 500 because RegisterIn Pydantic model is missing team and designation fields, while the endpoint body accesses body.team and body.designation — AttributeError: 'RegisterIn' object has no attribute 'team'. (2) /api/score still uses the OLD schema (ScoreIn.content_type: ContentType literal) — the spec said it should accept free-text request_type. Sending request_type fails with 422 missing content_type; sending content_type='blog_article' triggers a separate 500 (likely downstream LLM/agent). All other iteration-3 features (assigned_user_id required + denormalized fields on /submissions, auto_approve bypass, /accept/approve/request-revision specific-user gating, escalate with optional assigned_user_id + auto-pick same team, nudge to specific user, propose-timeline notifies the other specific party, scheduler creates notification only for assigned_user_id) work correctly. forward-to-ceo does NOT enforce assigned_user_id gating (only checks role==vp) — spec violation.

critical.issue: 500 AttributeError: RegisterIn has no attribute 'team'. The endpoint reads body.team and body.designation but the Pydantic model (server.py lines 144-148) does not declare them. Add `team: Optional[str] = ''` and `designation: Optional[str] = ''` to RegisterIn. Pre-seeded users already in DB via prior iterations work via /auth/login, masking the bug at startup.

Schema NOT migrated to request_type. ScoreIn still has `content_type: ContentType` (literal of 8 values). Sending the new payload {request_type: 'internal_newsletter', ...} returns 422 'content_type missing'. The spec explicitly required free-text request_type on /score. Replace ScoreIn.content_type with `request_type: str` and update the LLM prompt template accordingly (the prompt already references Request Type at line 314 so only the model field is wrong).

Does NOT enforce assigned_user_id gating. Code only checks `user['role'] != 'vp'` (line 658-659). Any VP can forward any submission, even one assigned to a different VP. Spec: VP-only AND must be the assigned user. Add: `if item.get('assigned_user_id') and user['id'] != item['assigned_user_id']: raise HTTPException(403, ...)`.

minor.issue: When the old content_type literal is sent, the endpoint returns 500 (likely the LLM/extract_json path failing) instead of a clean 4xx. After the request_type migration this becomes moot.

When chosen_tier=auto_approve, request_type is still required and submission stores assigned_user_id=None, assigned_user_name=None, assigned_user_email=None — verify analytics aggregations tolerate None for these fields.
Body is EscalateIn but if request omits body, FastAPI rejects with 422. Consider defaulting body to EscalateIn() (Optional Body) so escalate without arguments still works as documented.

action_items: Add `team: Optional[str] = ''` and `designation: Optional[str] = ''` fields to RegisterIn model in server.py (~line 144).
Replace `content_type: ContentType` in ScoreIn with `request_type: str` (free-text) — spec says /score should use request_type like /submissions.
Add assigned_user_id gating to /forward-to-ceo (currently only role-checked, allowing any VP to forward submissions assigned to other VPs).

Backfill team/designation on pre-seeded users — verified: submitter=Marketing/Marketing Associate, reviewer=Product/Senior Product Manager, lead=Marketing/Head of Marketing, vp=Marketing/VP of Marketing, ceo=Executive/Chief Executive Officer all returned correctly by /auth/me, /auth/login, /users (assuming users were created in a prior iteration with these fields; no fresh-register path tested due to the RegisterIn bug above).
critical_code_review_comments: RegisterIn Pydantic model is out of sync with the register handler — schema must declare every field the handler reads. This is a basic class of bug; consider adding a unit test that POSTs /auth/register with a fresh email on every CI run.
ScoreIn was not updated when the submission schema migrated to request_type. Two places carry the same conceptual field — keep them in sync (consider one shared ContentRequest model).

forward-to-ceo gating omission is a logic-level oversight: every other action endpoint (accept/approve/request-revision/escalate) has the assigned_user_id check, but forward-to-ceo doesn't. Add a shared helper `_require_assigned(item, user)` to enforce this uniformly.

_pick_next_assignee returns the first user found via to_list(100) with no deterministic ordering — fine functionally but tests asserting which user is chosen could flake. Add an explicit .sort('created_at', 1) for stability.

server.py is now 1167 lines — the 700-line threshold was already flagged in iteration 2 and grew further. Split into routers (auth, scoring, submissions, notifications, scheduler) now before more drift accumulates.

EscalateIn has no validation that assigned_user_id is non-empty if provided — empty string would currently fall to the auto-pick branch via Optional. Acceptable but worth pydantic constraints (min_length=1).
test_credentials: submitter@govern.app / reviewer@govern.app / lead@govern.app / vp@govern.app / ceo@govern.app — all Pass1234! (pre-existing in DB with correct team/designation; new registrations BROKEN until RegisterIn fix is applied)
seed_data_creation: Created ~20 TEST_IT3_* prefixed submissions covering assignment/gating flows. Most are pending_acceptance or in_progress and will continue to accumulate notifications until terminal states.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After E1 fixes the 3 issues (RegisterIn fields, ScoreIn request_type, forward-to-ceo gating), re-run /app/backend/tests/test_iteration3_features.py. The 7 failing tests are: test_register_accepts_team_designation, test_register_defaults_team_designation_empty (both blocked on RegisterIn fix), test_score_with_request_type_succeeds, test_score_with_old_content_type_fails (both blocked on ScoreIn fix), test_accept_blocks_other_users_even_same_role + test_escalate_to_specific_user + test_forward_to_ceo_blocks_non_assigned_vp (all blocked by RegisterIn — they register alt users mid-test). Iteration 1/2 test files (test_content_approval.py, test_iteration2_features.py) still use the old /score and /submissions schemas and will need rewrites before they can serve as regression — they were not re-run in this iteration.
rca of the issue: RegisterIn missing fields: classic Pydantic v2 behavior where unknown fields are silently dropped, so body.team raises AttributeError when accessed. RCA path: /auth/register POST → Pydantic validates RegisterIn (succeeds, drops team/designation) → handler accesses body.team → AttributeError → 500. Reproduction: curl -X POST $URL/api/auth/register -d '{"email":"x@y.com","password":"Pass1234!","name":"X","team":"Q","designation":"E"}'. Fix: add `team: Optional[str] = ''; designation: Optional[str] = ''` to RegisterIn (~line 148). ScoreIn: same root cause class — the model wasn't updated when the spec changed; the LLM prompt was. Fix: change ScoreIn.content_type to request_type: str.
